slides.title: Brand Identity at a Glance
body: Precise
Authoritative
Intelligent
Urgent
Premium
Visual style: dark editorial enterprise with AI-native identity
First impression: controlled confidence — you need this now

slides.title: Core Positioning
body: Positioning: AI-native data security platform for enterprises securing cloud, SaaS, on-prem, and AI simultaneously
Category play: DSPM (Data Security Posture Management) expanding to own AI Security before competitors pivot
Wedge message: AI breaks your old defenses — new challenges require intelligent responses
Primary value prop: One platform to discover, classify, govern, and protect data across every environment
Secondary value prop: Speed and scale — auto-remediate risks with one click, not weeks of manual work

slides.title: ICP & Buyer Profile
slides.subtitle: Who they are selling to and how they speak to them
body: Primary buyer: CISO, VP Security, Head of Data Security at enterprise companies (1000+ employees)
Industries targeted: Financial Services, Healthcare, Manufacturing, Federal, Retail, Tech Services
Company stage/size: Enterprise — inferred from demo-only CTA, no pricing, Paramount case study
Buyer sophistication: Dual-track — executive-simple hero, technical depth in DSPM/classification sub-pages
Pain trigger: AI adoption creating unknown sensitive data exposure before compliance or legal discovers it
Objection pre-empted: Speed and scale — they answer how fast before you ask if it actually works

slides.title: Messaging Architecture
body: H1: Protect your data. Secure AI.
Key H2: AI breaks your old defenses. New challenges require intelligent responses.
Key H2: One platform to secure AI — from data to access to action.
Key H2: Protect and comply at scale by making data and AI security highly actionable.
Sub-headline: Traditional classification relies on static rules and lacks context

slides.title: Messaging Framework Deep Dive
slides.subtitle: How they structure the narrative from top to bottom
body: Hook (above fold): Bold 2-line H1 — first line is product action, second is the AI category claim
Problem frame: AI is the threat actor — it made your old defenses obsolete, this is not your fault
Solution introduction: One unified AI-native platform — positions as category leap, not incremental upgrade
Proof point: Paramount customer story — billions of sensitive records identified, real enterprise at scale
Feature-to-benefit: DSPM → Auto-remediate risks quickly; Access Trail → Take informed actions with one click
Closing argument: Secure the unknown — reframes fear as opportunity, CTA is Get a Demo

slides.title: GTM Motion & CTA Strategy
body: GTM motion: Enterprise SLG (Sales-Led) — no self-serve, no pricing, demo-first confirms account-based selling
Primary CTA: Get a Demo → /demo — pure sales qualification, signals 30-90 day enterprise sales cycle
Secondary CTA: Assess your AI Security → /pulse-check/ai-security — top-of-funnel lead magnet
Lead magnet: AI Security Pulse Check — free assessment warms ICPs before they are ready for demo
Trust signals: Paramount case study placed before conversion; logos implied via industry nav segmentation
Friction level: High — 10+ field demo form signals intent-qualified leads only, not volume conversion play

slides.title: Visual Identity
body: Illustration style: Abstract geometric data-viz — network nodes, classification layers, data flows
Why this style: Makes invisible security tangible without showing UI — speaks to both exec and technical buyer
Animation intent: Ambient intelligence — Lottie loops make security feel alive, not static or corporate
Hero hierarchy: Text-first — H1 lands before illustration earns attention, correct for B2B executive buyers
Product abstraction: Full metaphor — no UI screenshots in hero, interface revealed only in demo funnel

slides.title: Animation Stack
slides.subtitle: How it is built technically
body: Primary stack: GSAP + GSAP ScrollTrigger + GSAP SplitText + Lottie + Swiper — built on Webflow
Scroll animations: ScrollTrigger snap — elements reveal as they enter viewport, 70% of all animations
Load animations: Hero entrance sequence — nav fade → H1 word reveal → CTA fade up → Lottie activates
Text animation: Word-level via GSAP SplitText — headlines split into words, staggered at ~80-100ms apart
Easing: cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1) — Material Design standard — smooth and readable, no spring physics

slides.title: 3 Animation Techniques to Steal
body: 1. SplitText word reveal: H2 headlines split word-by-word on scroll — creates revelation effect; implement with gsap.registerPlugin(SplitText) + ScrollTrigger snap
2. Lavender hover feedback: Brand color fills interactive surfaces on hover at .4s ease — makes interactions feel premium; implement with CSS transition: background-color .4s on any clickable element
3. Dark wedge section: Contrasting dark section immediately below hero reframes the problem — visitor keeps scrolling; implement with ScrollTrigger toggleClass on a dark bg section after the hero

slides.title: Illustration System Deep Dive
body: Style: Abstract geometric network — nodes, data flows, classification layers, no humans
Custom or library: Custom — bespoke variable fonts and named color tokens confirm designed-from-scratch system
Built for animation: Yes — Lottie files built in After Effects with named pre-comps for independent layer control
Color in illustrations: 5-7 hues, all from brand token system — helio-purple, lavender, pale-green on dark-violet
Depth technique: Opacity-layered pale-purple fills over dark-violet base — no shadows, pure transparency stack

slides.title: What to Steal / What to Avoid
body: ✓ Steal: Dual-CTA architecture — primary demo CTA + free assessment lead magnet meets buyers at two funnel stages
✓ Steal: Deep violet + heliotrope color system — distinct from every blue enterprise SaaS competitor in the category
✓ Steal: AI breaks your old defenses wedge section — naming the threat before introducing the solution drives scroll momentum
✗ Avoid: 10+ field demo form — even in enterprise this kills conversion; 3-field + Clearbit enrichment performs better
✗ Avoid: SplitText with missing spaces in scraped output — headline legibility breaks on mobile if spaces drop

slides.title: How to Replicate This Design System
slides.subtitle: Minimum viable build to get 80% of the visual impact
body: Style rule: Abstract geometric shapes representing data flow — no humans, no literal UI, curves preferred over sharp angles
Color system: 1 dark anchor (#160923) + 1 luminous glow (#d390ff) + 2 pale tints (lavender, pale-purple) + 1 success green
Animation rule: Lottie for hero (looping ambient), GSAP ScrollTrigger snap for reveals, nothing faster than 300ms
Tools: Figma for SVG design, After Effects + Bodymovin for Lottie export, GSAP Club for ScrollTrigger + SplitText
Build first: One hero Lottie animation showing data nodes in motion — this alone delivers 60% of the total visual impact

slides.title: Competitive Signals
body: Category owned: DSPM — entered existing category but racing to rebrand it as AI Security before Wiz or Varonis do
Benchmark 1: Wiz — mirrors the dark premium aesthetic and one-platform narrative, differentiates on data vs. cloud posture
Benchmark 2: Varonis — same data security buyer, differentiates on AI-native speed vs. Varonis legacy architecture
JTBD framing: Help me prove to the board we are AI-ready without exposing the company to a data breach
Differentiation signal: Only platform that secures both data AND AI pipelines in a single unified surface — no competitor claims both

slides.title: Design–Copy Harmony
body: Illustration reinforces headline: Yes — abstract network visualization makes Protect your data feel tangible, not abstract
Metaphor system: Visibility and illumination — data moves from dark/unknown to classified/safe across every section
Color as narrative: Dark violet (threat) → heliotrope (intelligence) → pale green (resolved) maps the emotional arc
Icon/illustration consistency: Lottie for features, SVG for UI chrome — unified by purple token system across both
Design principle this site nails: Illustrations replace product screenshots — buyers project their own infra onto abstract visuals

slides.quote: Cyera is the AI-native data security platform built for a world where AI makes old defenses obsolete — targeting enterprise security leaders who need to govern data and AI risk simultaneously from a single intelligent platform.
